body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) aims to list 407 securities, including 66 initial public offerings (IPOs), by 2025, despite global economic uncertainty and high interest rates, with companies having alternative funding options such as debt securities and banking facilities. Three prospective issuers from the basic materials, energy, and health sectors, with market capitalization above IDR 3 trillion, are expected to go public this year, adding to the 20 companies already in the pipeline.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange targets 407 listings, including 66 IPOs, despite global economic uncertainty.

body: PT Bank Indonesia Tbk (BOII) aims to increase lending by 15% year-over-year, targeting IDR 4.74 trillion in loan disbursement by the end of 2025, driven by a simpler and more efficient lending system focused on the retail sector. Despite challenges in cheap funds and loan-to-deposit ratio, BOII is confident of achieving 100% net profit growth by the end of 2025, with net profit already up 44.74% year-to-date to IDR 70.68 billion as of November 2024.
title: Bank of India Indonesia sets ambitious 2025 targets, aiming for 15% year-on-year loan growth and 100% net profit increase.

body: PT Solusi Kemasan Digital Tbk (PACK) has received approval from shareholders for a rights issue plan to improve its working capital, allowing the company to issue up to 100 billion new shares, which will increase its authorized capital to IDR 3 trillion from IDR 49.18 billion. As a result, the company's market capitalization stood at IDR 1.04 trillion, with its stock price increasing by IDR 60 to IDR 675 in early 2025.
title: PT Solusi Kemasan Digital receives shareholder approval for rights issue to increase working capital.

body: Indonesia's inflation rate in December 2024 slowed down to 1.57% year-on-year, but increased 0.02% month-to-month, driven by rises in most expenditure categories, including food, healthcare, and education. The December 2024 inflation rate was the lowest in the past couple of years, with the highest annual inflation rate recorded in Highland Papua at 5.36% and the lowest in North Sulawesi at 0.44%.
title: Indonesia's inflation rate slows down to 1.57% in December 2024, but increases month-to-month.

body: PT Karya Media Investindo acquired 3.30 billion shares of PT MDTV Media Technologies TBK (MDTV), increasing its ownership stake to 8%, while Sabana Prawira Widjaja boosted his stake in PT Campina Ice Cream Industry TBK (CAMI) to 83.94% by adding 2.35 billion shares. Meanwhile, PT HP Capital Resources sold 703.11 million shares of PT Trisula Textile Industries TBK (TRIS), reducing its stake from 14.86% to 5.16%, and other investors made notable transactions in various companies, including PT MD Pictures TBK, PT Provident Investasi Bersama TBK, and PT Yulie Sekuritas Indonesia TBK.
title: Multiple investors engage in significant share buying and selling transactions in various Indonesian companies.

body: PT Erajaya Swasembada Tbk (ERAA) is expanding into the electric vehicle industry through its affiliate PT Era Inovasi Otomotif, which has partnered with XPeng to establish an electric vehicle ecosystem in Indonesia, expected to generate financial contributions to the company. The cooperation agreement was signed with Shenzen Xiaopeng Motors Supply Chain Management Co Ltd and Guangzhou Xiaopeng Motors Trading Co Ltd, marking ERAA's entry into the electric vehicle market.
title: Erajaya expands into electric vehicle industry through partnership with Chinese brand XPeng.

body: Indonesia's Finance Minister, Sri Mulyani Indrawati, expressed optimism for the country's economic growth in 2024, despite the Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) facing pressure from various factors, including elections, and hitting its lowest point in 2024. The JCI closed 0.62% stronger at 7,079.90 on the last trading day of 2024, according to IDNFinancials data.
title: Minister of Finance Sri Mulyani Indrawati opens first trading session of the year at Indonesia Stock Exchange, conveying President's hopes for economic growth and stability.

body: PT Raharja Energi Cepu TBK (RATU) has set an initial public offering (IPO) price of IDR 1,150 per share, aiming to raise IDR 624.46 billion from the sale of 543.01 million shares, or 20% of its total issued and paid-up capital post-IPO. The majority of the IPO proceeds will be allocated to loans for subsidiaries, including IDR 157.36 billion for PT Raharja Energi Tanjung Jabung and IDR 34.97 billion for PT Petrogas Jatim Utama Cendana, with the remainder used for working capital.
title: PT Raharja Energi Cepu Sets IPO Price at IDR 1,150 per Share to Raise IDR 624.46 Billion.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) saw gains in the morning session of the first trading day of 2025, climbing to 7,128.92 from 7,092.43 at the opening bell, driven by positive shifts in several big market capitalization companies in the energy and banking sectors. Banking issuers such as PT Bank Central Asia Tbk, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk, and PT Bank Mandiri Tbk, as well as energy companies like PT Alamtri Resources Indonesia Tbk and PT Pertamina Gas Negara Tbk, saw collective uptrends, contributing to the JCI's uptrend.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Rises on First Trading Day of 2025 Led by Gains in Banking and Energy Sectors

body: PT Yelooo Data Integra Tbk (YLOO) aims to achieve a revenue target of IDR 325.32 billion by 2025, representing a 9% increase from its projected 2024 revenue of IDR 294.14 billion. To reach this goal, the company is implementing various strategies, including cooperation with travel agents, data sales, fibre optic network development, and e-sim card sales, after experiencing a decline in revenue and profit in September 2024 compared to the same period in 2023.
title: PT Yelooo Data Integra to Implement Various Strategies to Achieve IDR 325.32 Billion Revenue Target by 2025.

body: PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) reached a market capitalization of IDR 1.18 quadrillion in December 2024, a 2.87% increase from IDR 1.14 quadrillion in December 2023, solidifying its position as the top market capitalization leader on the stock market exchange. The bank's transaction volume stood at 56.35 million shares, worth IDR 547.27 billion, with a closing price of IDR 9,675 per share, IDR 275 higher than its December 29, 2023, closing position.
title: Bank Central Asia's market capitalization reaches IDR 1.18 quadrillion in December 2024, ranking top in market capitalization leaderboard.

body: PT Indocement Tunggal Prakarsa Tbk (INDO) has completed its shares buyback program, spending IDR 565.05 billion to repurchase 81.09 million shares at an average price of IDR 6.967 each, equivalent to 2.20% of its total shares listed on the Indonesia Stock Exchange. The company has IDR 329.94 billion remaining from its allocated budget, which will likely be used to support future financial operational strategies and maintain shareholder value and price stability.
title: Indocement concludes share buyback program, repurchasing 81.09 million shares worth IDR 565.05 billion.
